evidence_quotes.quote: there is a recurring pattern in which the biggest companies, constantly prey on customers worse impulses for money
The next step would be to regulate gambling in games, but that won't be fighting over pennies so it will take longer even if its even more important.
pull the rug out of their feet and close their servers, making the games unplayable and rendering what they sold worthless.

paragraphs.text: A company will not protect the interest of its customers, there is a recurring pattern in which the biggest companies, constantly prey on customers worse impulses for money only to pull the rug out of their feet and close their servers, making the games unplayable and rendering what they sold worthless. At the very least the customers need to be protected in this regard, they should be able to keep what they paid for. Games will still be clearly profitable, this is a minor nuisance for companies and a huge problem for the consumers. Also, most gamers tend to be more apolitical so it would be a gesture that could have a great effect in changing the image of a politician. I myself have not voted in the last Spain election and yet I'm writing feedback in favor of this initiative, so yeah. People in the games industry are shortsightedly pushing to uphold the status quo, worsening the health of the industry for a quick buck, so the politicians who are able to display common sense and recognize the popular initiatives are the ones who will last. The next step would be to regulate gambling in games, but that won't be fighting over pennies so it will take longer even if its even more important.
